keyTakeaway: Formula: **P(n,k) = n!/(n-k)!**. Direct calculation from your input values. Counts the number of ways to select k items from n where order matters.

explanation:
Mathematics is a universal tool: algebra, geometry, calculus, statistics. Applied in engineering, science, finance, and everyday life.

## How Permutations Work

Permutations count the number of ways to arrange or select k items from n items where order matters. This calculator applies the formula with your input values, checking for reasonable ranges and alerting you to unusual inputs.

## Important Notes

This calculation is for reference purposes. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a specialized professional. All formulas are current through 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: What's the difference between permutations and combinations?
faq.a: Permutations count ordered arrangements (AB ≠ BA). If order doesn't matter, use combinations instead.

faq.q: What happens when k equals n?
faq.a: P(n,n) = n! (the factorial of n), because you're arranging all n items.
For repetition allowed, use n^k (called n-tuples).

faq.q: Can you give me a real-world example?
faq.a: Select 3 different digits from 0–9 for a code: P(10,3) = 720 unique codes.

faq.q: How does P(n,k) relate to combinations?
faq.a: The relationship is P(n,k) = C(n,k) × k! (combinations times the factorial of k).
